div.p: Version: 1.0.4 | Engine: ELK | Scope: Runtime Execution

ul.li: All local variables are dropped in reverse order.
If a JoinHandle exists, result is propagated.
Memory is reclaimed by the slab allocator.

g.title: Task is in the global/local run queue, awaiting scheduler dispatch.
Task is currently assigned to a CPU core/Worker thread.
Task is suspended awaiting an external event or synchronization primitive.
Terminal state: Normal return. Resources scheduled for drop.
Terminal state: Uncaught exception. Unwinding stack.

g.text: 1. resume() - Context switch to coroutine stack
3. await I/O - Suspended, waker stored in reactor
4. I/O ready - Reactor wakes task into Runnable queue
5. return - Final cleanup and dropping futures
Runnable → Finished (ILLEGAL: Must execute at least 1 poll)
Runnable → Panicked (ILLEGAL: Panic occurs during execution)
Blocked → Running (ILLEGAL: Must be re-queued in Runnable first)
Runnable: Task is present in LIFO/FIFO run-queue
Running: Associated with exactly one OS Thread/Worker
Blocked: Pointer registered in Reactor/Epoll instance
Finished: Memory deallocated or moved to join-handle
Panicked: Stack unwound, lock poisoning active

g.title: Task is in the global/local run queue, awaiting scheduler dispatch.
Task is currently assigned to a CPU core/Worker thread.
Task is suspended awaiting an external event or synchronization primitive.
Terminal state: Normal return. Resources scheduled for drop.
Terminal state: Uncaught exception. Unwinding stack.
